title: holosteum umbellatum subsp. hirsutum
intro: Holosteum umbellatum subsp. hirsutum, also known as Hairy Beggarticks, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae. It is an annual herb with hairy stems and leaves, and produces small white or pinkish flowers in clusters. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant effects. The health benefits of Holosteum umbellatum subsp. hirsutum include relieving respiratory issues such as coughs and colds, as well as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, and it can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture.
